pack.name: Bash
pack.description: Essential Bash shell scripting and command-line workflows
pack.author: sherpacli-community

commands.name: Enable strict mode
commands.description: Enable safer Bash defaults for scripts.
commands.use_case: Use at the top of Bash scripts to fail fast and avoid silent errors.

commands.name: Run script file
commands.use_case: Use when running a script without changing executable permissions.

commands.name: Check file exists
commands.use_case: Use in scripts before reading or modifying files.

examples.command: if [[ -e config.env ]]; then cat config.env; fi

commands.name: Loop over range
commands.description: Basic numeric for-loop over a range.
commands.command: for i in {1..5}; do echo "$i"; done
commands.use_case: Use for repeated command execution with small numeric ranges.

examples.command: for i in {1..5}; do echo "Welcome $i"; done

commands.name: Read file line by line
commands.description: Process each line from a file.
commands.command: while read -r line; do echo "$line"; done < file.txt
commands.use_case: Use for line-safe parsing without word-splitting bugs.

examples.command: while read -r line; do printf '%s\n' "$line"; done < input.txt

commands.name: Use command substitution
commands.use_case: Use to embed command output in scripts and logs.

commands.name: Default parameter value
commands.description: Use fallback value when variable is unset.
commands.use_case: Use to make scripts resilient when env vars are missing.

commands.name: Redirect stderr to stdout
commands.use_case: Use when capturing full command output in one log file.

commands.name: Prompt for input
commands.description: Read user input into a variable.
commands.use_case: Use in interactive scripts for confirmations or parameters.
